header.description: Spitfire Mk V flies a banked climbing spiral: roll in to 45 deg at g=1/cos(45) (level coordinated turn), then pull to g=1.7 so the lift both turns AND lifts the nose (excess over the level value n*cos(phi)>cos(gamma) -> climb). B2: the climb is earned in the pitch plane and the harder pull bleeds more speed (induced drag). Start alt 3000 m stays clear of the ceiling; gamma stays well inside +/-90 deg.
